new_classes.label_en: other still wine of fresh grapes, in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2204 21 98)
new_classes.label_de: anderer Stillwein aus frischen Weintrauben, in Behältnissen bis 2 L (KN 2204 21 98)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, bearing neither a protected designation of origin (PDO) nor a protected geographical indication (PGI), classified under CN 2204 21 98. The non-volatile constituents of the total dry extract include sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts, as defined in additional note 4(A) to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation:
The proposed axioms capture the four primary distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 21 98:
1. ✅ Wine of fresh grapes (parent class WineOfFreshGrapes).
2. ✅ Container volume ≤ 2 L (SmallContainer part restriction).
3. ✅ No PDO (complement of ProtectedDesignationOfOriginQuality).
4. ✅ No PGI (complement of ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality).
5. ✅ Still/non-sparkling wine (complement of CarbonationQuality).

Gaps reducing the score:
- The legal text references additional notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22, including specific dry extract thresholds (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter, salts). These compositional/analytical criteria (total dry extract non-volatile constituents) are not currently expressible in the TBox without introducing new quality classes and datatype properties for each constituent — overly granular for the current ontology level. These criteria are not modelled (approx. −0.20 coverage penalty).
- The "other" character (residual category after 22042111–22042196 siblings) is implied structurally by the complement restrictions but cannot be exhaustively enumerated at this axiomatisation level.
